stem: A agriculture study measures the yield per hectare (tonnes) for a sample of 40 farms. The sample mean is 87.6 tonnes with a sample standard deviation of 20.1 tonnes. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 87.6 tonnes. 95% CI = [81.2, 94.0]. Point estimate: 87.6 tonnes.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
